danger_signals.visual_form: Defeated boss pictures shown in post-level cutscenes
danger_signals.meaning: Threat overcome; progression marker for narrative danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Level design obstacles (implied through stage progression)
danger_signals.meaning: Physical danger to player character during platforming

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual health meter (accessible via debug command)
Weapons and spear collectibles visible in gameplay

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Third eye (cross-shaped bandage covers Sharaku's forehead in normal state; third eye represents hidden evil genius and superhuman intellect)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ancient ruins and mysterious locations (Easter Island, Arizona, Mexico); Moai statues as environmental storytelling elements

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red Condor (magical weapon controlled via three-eyed script phrase)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss enemies at stage conclusions; depicted in post-level cutscene artwork

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cross-shaped bandage on forehead; childlike appearance; dumb, obedient demeanor
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vulnerable, bullied by peers; requires support from Chiyoko Wato; passive interaction mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Third eye visible on forehead; malicious expression implied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High intelligence emerges; evil genius behavior; psychic powers activate; can control Red Condor weapon; becomes adversarial to authority figures

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Defeated boss picture displayed; cutscene text appears describing location narrative (e.g., Easter Island arrival)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sharaku advances investigation; moves to next ruin location; story escalates through visual narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental tileset changes per stage; mysterious architecture; artifact presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Puzzle-solving mode; interaction with three-eyed civilization artifacts; deciphering ancient scripts

environmental_storytelling.technique: Post-level cutscene narration with defeated boss imagery
environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 4 ending: boss picture shown with Japanese text; Stage 5 (unused): Moai statues at Easter Island depicted as seemingly-harmless but actually hostile entities, emphasizing how relics of the Three-Eyed civilization manifest as threats

Stages set in real-world archaeological mystery sites (Easter Island, Arizona, Mexico); each location ties Sharaku's investigation to actual historical mysteries, grounding the three-eyed civilization lore in tangible places

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character visual identity tied to mechanical state
environmental_storytelling.example: Bandaged forehead = passive child; revealed third eye = active superhuman. This visual toggle communicates internal power state and narrative significance without dialog
Red Condor as signature three-eyed civilization weapon; controlled via ancient script incantation, marking it as non-human technology and reinforcing alien/ancient faction identity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Childish, obedient, bullied, clueless; dependent on Chiyoko Wato for guidance and protection
Malicious, intellectually superior, exhibits psychic powers, solves/creates problems, investigates ruins independently, controls Red Condor
Acts as mother figure, love interest, and stabilizing force; tomboyish self-confidence suggests capable ally during investigation sequences
Present major threat; visual depiction in post-defeat cutscene marks progression; implied to be obstacles guarding ruins or opposing Sharaku's investigation
Initially appear dormant/non-threatening ('thought to be only statues'); suddenly become hostile ('mounted an attack'); subverts player expectation about ancient artifact danger

notes: Mitsume ga Tooru is a narrative-driven platformer where visual communication of world-state is heavily dependent on post-level cutscenes rather than real-time in-game iconography. The core mechanic—Sharaku's sealed third eye as a visual toggle—directly links character appearance to ability state and thematic duality (innocent child vs. evil genius). Environmental storytelling exploits real archaeological locations to ground the fantastical three-eyed civilization premise. Limited direct faction conflict is visible in-game; most political/narrative tension conveyed through cutscene stills and Japanese text. Unused Stage 5 cutscene data suggests expanded environmental storytelling around Easter Island ruins was planned but removed due to memory constraints.
